{
  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “Check this,” which focus do you want: A) writing polish and clarity only (R1.6), B) substantive content check for logic and evidence-claim alignment (R1.2, R1.3), C) novelty/positioning vs prior work and citation considerations (R1.1, R1.4, R1.7), or D) all of the above with B prioritized?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A logical-consistency pass would examine whether conclusions like the debate dynamic driving gains follow coherently from the premises and described ablations.",
    "R1.3": "An evidence-claim alignment check would verify that the reported F1 improvements and ablation results substantively support the stated claims about performance and mechanisms.",
    "R1.6": "A writing-polish pass would target sentence-level grammar, clarity, and register within this abstract-style excerpt.",
    "RX.1": "We will explicitly note uncertainties and limits (e.g., dataset scope and small transcript sample) when framing any feedback.",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid endorsing or amplifying overbroad claims (e.g., generalizability or production readiness) beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "We will confine the check to the provided excerpt and not infer or require details from the rest of the paper.",
    "RX.4": "Given the clinical context, we will flag any wording that could lead to unsafe interpretations or misapplication in care settings.",
    "RX.5": "We will ground observations in specific sentences and numbers from the excerpt to maintain traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “Check this,” which focus do you want: A) writing polish and clarity only (R1.6), B) substantive content check for logic and evidence-claim alignment (R1.2, R1.3), C) novelty/positioning vs prior work and citation considerations (R1.1, R1.4, R1.7), or D) all of the above with B prioritized?

rationale.R1.2: A logical-consistency pass would examine whether conclusions like the debate dynamic driving gains follow coherently from the premises and described ablations.
rationale.R1.3: An evidence-claim alignment check would verify that the reported F1 improvements and ablation results substantively support the stated claims about performance and mechanisms.
rationale.R1.6: A writing-polish pass would target sentence-level grammar, clarity, and register within this abstract-style excerpt.
rationale.RX.1: We will explicitly note uncertainties and limits (e.g., dataset scope and small transcript sample) when framing any feedback.
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid endorsing or amplifying overbroad claims (e.g., generalizability or production readiness) beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: We will confine the check to the provided excerpt and not infer or require details from the rest of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Given the clinical context, we will flag any wording that could lead to unsafe interpretations or misapplication in care settings.
rationale.RX.5: We will ground observations in specific sentences and numbers from the excerpt to maintain traceability.
